speaker: CHARLES
text: Mars his true moving, even as in the heavens

speaker: CHARLES
text: So in the Earth, to this day is not known.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Late did he shine upon the English side;

speaker: CHARLES
text: Now we are victors; upon us he smiles.

speaker: CHARLES
text: What towns of any moment but we have?

speaker: CHARLES
text: At pleasure here we lie, near Orleance.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Otherwhiles, the famished English, like pale ghosts,

speaker: CHARLES
text: Faintly besiege us one hour in a month.

speaker: ALANSON
text: They want their porridge and their fat bull beeves.

speaker: ALANSON
text: Either they must be dieted like mules

speaker: ALANSON
text: And have their provender tied to their mouths,

speaker: ALANSON
text: Or piteous they will look, like drownèd mice.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Let’s raise the siege. Why live we idly here?

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Talbot is taken, whom we wont to fear.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Remaineth none but mad-brained Salisbury,

speaker: REIGNIER
text: And he may well in fretting spend his gall;

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Nor men nor money hath he to make war.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Sound, sound alarum! We will rush on them.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Now for the honor of the forlorn French!

speaker: CHARLES
text: Him I forgive my death that killeth me

speaker: CHARLES
text: When he sees me go back one foot, or fly.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Whoever saw the like? What men have I!

speaker: CHARLES
text: Dogs, cowards, dastards! I would ne’er have fled

speaker: CHARLES
text: But that they left me ’midst my enemies.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Salisbury is a desperate homicide.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: He fighteth as one weary of his life.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: The other lords, like lions wanting food,

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Do rush upon us as their hungry prey.

speaker: ALANSON
text: Froissart, a countryman of ours, records

speaker: ALANSON
text: England all Olivers and Rolands bred

speaker: ALANSON
text: During the time Edward the Third did reign.

speaker: ALANSON
text: More truly now may this be verified,

speaker: ALANSON
text: For none but Samsons and Goliases

speaker: ALANSON
text: It sendeth forth to skirmish. One to ten!

speaker: ALANSON
text: Lean rawboned rascals! Who would e’er suppose

speaker: ALANSON
text: They had such courage and audacity?

speaker: CHARLES
text: Let’s leave this town, for they are hare-brained slaves,

speaker: CHARLES
text: And hunger will enforce them to be more eager.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Of old I know them; rather with their teeth

speaker: CHARLES
text: The walls they’ll tear down than forsake the siege.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: I think by some odd gimmers or device

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Their arms are set, like clocks, still to strike on;

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Else ne’er could they hold out so as they do.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: By my consent, we’ll even let them alone.

speaker: ALANSON
text: Be it so.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Where’s the Prince Dauphin? I have news for him.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Bastard of Orleance, thrice welcome to us.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Methinks your looks are sad, your cheer appalled.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Hath the late overthrow wrought this offence?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Be not dismayed, for succor is at hand.

speaker: BASTARD
text: A holy maid hither with me I bring,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Which, by a vision sent to her from heaven,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Ordainèd is to raise this tedious siege

speaker: BASTARD
text: And drive the English forth the bounds of France.

speaker: BASTARD
text: The spirit of deep prophecy she hath,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Exceeding the nine Sibyls of old Rome.

speaker: BASTARD
text: What’s past and what’s to come she can descry.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Speak, shall I call her in? Believe my words,

speaker: BASTARD
text: For they are certain and unfallible.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Go call her in.

speaker: CHARLES
text: But first, to try her skill,

speaker: CHARLES
text: Reignier, stand thou as Dauphin in my place;

speaker: CHARLES
text: Question her proudly; let thy looks be stern.

speaker: CHARLES
text: By this means shall we sound what skill she hath.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Fair maid, is ’t thou wilt do these wondrous feats?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Reignier, is ’t thou that thinkest to beguile me?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Where is the Dauphin?—Come, come from behind.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: I know thee well, though never seen before.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Be not amazed; there’s nothing hid from me.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: In private will I talk with thee apart.—

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Stand back, you lords, and give us leave a while.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: She takes upon her bravely at first dash.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Dauphin, I am by birth a shepherd’s daughter,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: My wit untrained in any kind of art.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Heaven and Our Lady gracious hath it pleased

speaker: PUCELLE
text: To shine on my contemptible estate.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Lo, whilst I waited on my tender lambs,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And to sun’s parching heat displayed my cheeks,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: God’s Mother deignèd to appear to me,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And in a vision full of majesty

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Willed me to leave my base vocation

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And free my country from calamity.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Her aid she promised and assured success.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: In complete glory she revealed herself;

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And whereas I was black and swart before,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: With those clear rays which she infused on me

speaker: PUCELLE
text: That beauty am I blest with, which you may see.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Ask me what question thou canst possible,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And I will answer unpremeditated.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: My courage try by combat, if thou dar’st,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And thou shalt find that I exceed my sex.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Resolve on this: thou shalt be fortunate

speaker: PUCELLE
text: If thou receive me for thy warlike mate.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Thou hast astonished me with thy high terms.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Only this proof I’ll of thy valor make:

speaker: CHARLES
text: In single combat thou shalt buckle with me,

speaker: CHARLES
text: And if thou vanquishest, thy words are true;

speaker: CHARLES
text: Otherwise I renounce all confidence.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: I am prepared. Here is my keen-edged sword,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Decked with fine flower-de-luces on each side—

speaker: PUCELLE
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              The which at Touraine, in Saint Katherine’s churchyard,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Out of a great deal of old iron I chose forth.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Then come, a’ God’s name! I fear no woman.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And while I live, I’ll ne’er fly from a man.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Stay, stay thy hands! Thou art an Amazon,

speaker: CHARLES
text: And fightest with the sword of Deborah.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Christ’s mother helps me; else I were too weak.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Whoe’er helps thee, ’tis thou that must help me.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Impatiently I burn with thy desire.

speaker: CHARLES
text: My heart and hands thou hast at once subdued.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Excellent Pucelle, if thy name be so,

speaker: CHARLES
text: Let me thy servant and not sovereign be.

speaker: CHARLES
text: ’Tis the French Dauphin sueth to thee thus.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: I must not yield to any rights of love,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: For my profession’s sacred from above.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: When I have chasèd all thy foes from hence,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Then will I think upon a recompense.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Meantime look gracious on thy prostrate thrall.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: My lord, methinks, is very long in talk.

speaker: ALANSON
text: Doubtless he shrives this woman to her smock,

speaker: ALANSON
text: Else ne’er could he so long protract his speech.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Shall we disturb him, since he keeps no mean?

speaker: ALANSON
text: He may mean more than we poor men do know.

speaker: ALANSON
text: These women are shrewd tempters with their tongues.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: My lord, where are you? What devise you on?

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Shall we give o’er Orleance, or no?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Why, no, I say. Distrustful recreants,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Fight till the last gasp. I’ll be your guard.

speaker: CHARLES
text: What she says I’ll confirm: we’ll fight it out.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Assigned am I to be the English scourge.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: This night the siege assuredly I’ll raise.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Expect Saint Martin’s summer, halcyons’ days,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Since I have enterèd into these wars.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Glory is like a circle in the water,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Which never ceaseth to enlarge itself

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Till by broad spreading it disperse to naught.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: With Henry’s death, the English circle ends;

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Dispersèd are the glories it included.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Now am I like that proud insulting ship

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Which Caesar and his fortune bare at once.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Was Mahomet inspirèd with a dove?

speaker: CHARLES
text: Thou with an eagle art inspirèd then.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Helen, the mother of great Constantine,

speaker: CHARLES
text: Nor yet Saint Philip’s daughters were like thee.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Bright star of Venus, fall’n down on the Earth,

speaker: CHARLES
text: How may I reverently worship thee enough?

speaker: ALANSON
text: Leave off delays, and let us raise the siege.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Woman, do what thou canst to save our honors.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Drive them from Orleance and be immortalized.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Presently we’ll try. Come, let’s away about it.

speaker: CHARLES
text: No prophet will I trust if she prove false.
